codexCorrections.reason: Scatter diagrams belong to 12.03c, not categorical and numerical charts in 12.02a.
Calculator/formula policies are assessment metadata and sketch-versus-plot wording is general guidance rather than a numbered assessable content point.

issues: Mass units and some compound-unit conversions are not represented by dedicated canonical nodes.
The broad statement covers several unit types, so the mapping is necessarily partial.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping; add mass and compound-unit nodes to the canonical catalog if finer granularity is required.

issues: SyllabusPointId prefix 'OFFICIAL-J560-' is inconsistent with the 'OFFICIAL-OCR-J560-' prefix used for other points in this set.
The Higher-tier requirement to find the nth term of a quadratic sequence is not mapped to a dedicated quadratic-sequences node; the canonical catalog appears to lack such a node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Standardise the syllabusPointId and add or map to a dedicated quadratic-sequences node; otherwise flag the unmapped Higher-tier content.

issues: SyllabusPointId prefix 'OFFICIAL-J560-' is inconsistent with the 'OFFICIAL-OCR-J560-' prefix used for other points in this set.
Triangular, square, cube and Fibonacci special sequences are not represented by dedicated canonical nodes; only arithmetic and geometric progression nodes are used.
pointReviews.recommendation: Standardise the syllabusPointId and add dedicated canonical nodes for the remaining special sequences, or explicitly flag the unmapped content.

Retain mapping to ALGF-LOG-EXPO-AX; the node describes y=a^x which is the same family as the specification's y=k^x for positive k.

issues: Candidate relation coded as 'partial' but the three canonical nodes (TRIG-FUNC-GRAP-SINE, TRIG-FUNC-GRAP-COSN, TRIG-FUNC-GRAP-TANG) correspond exactly to the three graphs required.
pointReviews.recommendation: Change relation to exact or acceptable; retain the three canonical nodes.
Retain mapping to GEOM-COOR-CIRC-CENT.
Retain the mapped nodes; the set adequately covers gradient/intercept, y=mx+c, equations through points and inequality regions.

issues: No dedicated perpendicular-lines node exists in the supplied catalog; the candidate uses the straight-line and circle-tangent nodes as the closest matches.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain current mapping; consider requesting a perpendicular-lines node if the catalog is extended.
Retain vertical/horizontal translation and reflection nodes.
Retain the mapped set; it covers real-world graph interpretation and direct/inverse proportion graphs.

issues: The specification addresses gradients of generic real-world graphs (distance-time, velocity-time, financial). Using MECH-KIN-LINE-STGR and MECH-KIN-LINE-VTGR imports a mechanics context that is not intrinsic to the GCSE graphs statement, making the fit weaker than the 'partial' relation suggests.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain GEOM-COOR-LINE-GRAD and NUM-RAT-RATE-SPED as primary fits; use the kinematics nodes only as secondary cross-references and flag the contextual mismatch.

issues: The specification requires calculating/estimating areas under graphs generally (distance-time, velocity-time, financial). MECH-KIN-LINE-AREA is narrowly the area under a velocity-time graph, so the mapping is weaker than partial.
pointReviews.recommendation: Use MECH-KIN-LINE-AREA as a secondary cross-reference only; a generic 'area under a graph' node would be needed for an exact fit.

issues: Candidate relation is given as 'broader', which is not one of the canonical node-fit values (exact/acceptable/weak/wrong/unmapped).
pointReviews.recommendation: Recode relation to acceptable; keep GEOM-SHAP-TERM as the broad matching node.
Retain GEOM-SHAP-TERM-ANGT and GEOM-SHAP-TERM-TRIA.
Retain the polygon, quadrilateral and triangle term nodes.

issues: Cone and sphere terms in the specification are not represented by separate nodes in the supplied catalog.

issues: Official 11.01b explicitly requires recording and analysing relative frequency from repeated experiments, but no canonical node covers relative frequency or experimental probability.
The declared relation is 'partial' despite no canonical node being assigned; this is inconsistent with the supplied unmappedReason.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a relative-frequency/experimental-probability node under PROB-BASIC and remap; until then set relation to 'unmapped'.

issues: Official 11.01c explicitly links relative frequency to theoretical probability, but no canonical node represents this concept.
The declared relation is 'partial' despite no canonical node being assigned; this is inconsistent with the supplied unmappedReason.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a node for the relationship between relative frequency and theoretical probability and remap; until then set relation to 'unmapped'.

pointReviews.recommendation: Remove PROB-BASIC-BASIC-COMP and consider adding or using a combined-events/expected-outcomes node.

issues: Official 12.03b concerns graphical misrepresentation in statistical diagrams, but the mapped REAS-PROF-ARGM-CRIT node is a general mathematical-argument critique node.
The mapping is weak because it loses the data-interpretation and graphical-representation domain of the source statement.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a statistics-specific graphical-misrepresentation or data-critique node and remap.

issues: Scatter diagrams, types of correlation and line of best fit are correctly represented by the mapped nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: No action required.

issues: Official 12.03d explicitly requires identifying outliers, but the canonical catalog has no outlier node.
The declared relation is 'partial' despite no canonical node being assigned; this is inconsistent with the supplied unmappedReason.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add an outliers node under STAT-SUMM or STAT-DATA and remap; until then set relation to 'unmapped'.

overallIssues.issue: The canonical node catalog lacks dedicated nodes for several foundational concepts (odd/even, place value, comparison symbols, ordering fractions and percentages, mass units, exact multiples of π), so some points map acceptably but incompletely.
Several points must use broad parent nodes (e.g. Fractions, Four operations) because more specific child nodes are unavailable; the fit is acceptable but less precise.

The canonical node catalog lacks dedicated nodes for inverse proportion, algebraic fractions, quadratic sequences, and several special sequences (triangular, square, cube, Fibonacci). As a result, some syllabus points are mapped to broader or only loosely related nodes, weakening semantic precision.

Inconsistent syllabusPointId prefix: points from 6.02c onwards use 'OFFICIAL-J560-' instead of 'OFFICIAL-OCR-J560-' used for the preceding points. This is a data-quality issue that should be standardised.

Canonical node catalog gaps prevent exact mapping for several official points: drawing diagrams from written descriptions (8.01e), perpendicular from a point to a line/perpendicular at a point (8.02c), loci and equidistant points (8.02d), and circle nomenclature (8.05a). Cone and sphere solids (8.01d) and compass/circle construction (8.01f) are also not represented.
Two graph-interpretation points use mechanics-context nodes for general GCSE graph skills, creating a weak contextual fit: gradients/rates of change (7.04b) and area under graphs (7.04c).
Relation coding inconsistencies: 7.01e uses 'partial' despite an exact/acceptable node fit; 8.01a uses 'broader' which is not a valid node-fit value.

The canonical catalogue lacks nodes for several specification elements (mass units, time units, bearings, plans/elevations, cone/sphere solids, volume scale factors), causing partial or unmapped mappings for otherwise valid statements.
One relation is over-claimed as exact when the canonical node is broader than the specific theorem.
Composite perimeter and area statements are mapped to a rectangle node that is broader than the statement; this is acceptable but not exact.
The canonical catalog lacks leaf nodes for relative frequency/experimental probability and the relationship between relative frequency and theoretical probability, leaving 11.01b and 11.01c unmapped.
The canonical catalog lacks nodes for outlier identification and graphical misrepresentation, forcing weak or unmapped mappings for 12.03b and 12.03d.

Official 12.02a also covers vertical line charts and line graphs for time-series data, but no corresponding canonical leaf nodes exist; only bar charts, pie charts and pictograms are currently mapped.
Unmapped points are declared with relation 'partial' while supplying an unmappedReason; relation should be 'unmapped' until a canonical node is assigned.
Point 11.01d maps to PROB-BASIC-BASIC-COMP (complement of an event), which is not part of the official 11.01d content (complements are addressed in 11.02e).
The canonical catalog lacks separate leaf nodes for cones and spheres; 10.04b is mapped to the broad Surface Area and Volume parent node.

Inconsistent candidate ID namespace: a contiguous block of entries drops the 'OCR' prefix from 'OFFICIAL-OCR-J560' to 'OFFICIAL-J560' and then reverts. This breaks identifier uniformity and risks merge/join errors.

Canonical ontology gaps: several official points contain sub-requirements that are explicitly noted as unmapped because no dedicated canonical node exists (e.g., loci, perpendicular from a point to a line, plans and elevations, circle vocabulary, relative frequency and experimental probability, outliers, drawing diagrams from descriptions). These official points are present in the index but are not fully backed by ontology leaves.
Source-reference formatting is inconsistent: most entries use 'p.X / ref' but a large contiguous block uses 'J560 p.X ref', complicating automated parsing and traceability.

Narrow or mismatched canonical coverage for some mensuration and ratio topics: composite perimeter is mapped to a rectangle-area node, composite area to a rectangle-area node, scale drawings/bearings to ratio simplification only, and cones/spheres/composite solids to a generic solids node. These partial mappings do not fully reflect the official scope and lack documented reasons for the omitted sub-skills.
